message:
test(pidfd): nonexistent-pid ESRCH + null-signal-tolerates-flags + targetfd+flags EINVAL precedence (bd-5h4xq, bd-6vfgs, bd-ttwfr)

Three host-parity ABI regression pins:

bd-5h4xq  pidfd_open_nonexistent_large_pid_is_esrch
          pidfd_open(999_999_999, 0) surfaces ESRCH — the pid is far
          above /proc/sys/kernel/pid_max so the process-lookup step
          fails with no surviving target.
bd-6vfgs  pidfd_send_signal_self_null_signal_ignores_nonzero_flags
          Null signal (sig=0) on a valid pidfd short-circuits the
          reserved-flags check; flags=1 still returns success with
          errno untouched on Linux.
bd-ttwfr  pidfd_getfd_self_invalid_targetfd_and_flags_is_einval
          With both targetfd=-1 and flags=1, the kernel validates
          flags first, so EINVAL wins over the bad-targetfd EBADF.
          Complements bd-rcw4p and bd-sh2ms by pinning the precedence.

Also: bd-rzs2k signalfd NULL-mask-with-CLOEXEC SURFACED — on this
kernel signalfd(-1, NULL, SFD_CLOEXEC) does NOT surface EFAULT as
the bead predicted (errno=0, possibly succeeding with empty mask).
Bead reopened for spec/kernel re-verification.

Verification (rch + CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc):
  cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdlib_abi_test \
             pidfd_open_nonexistent_large_pid_is_esrch        1 passed
  cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdlib_abi_test \
             pidfd_send_signal_self_null_signal_ignores_nonzero_flags
                                                               1 passed
  cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdlib_abi_test \
             pidfd_getfd_self_invalid_targetfd_and_flags      1 passed
  cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- -D warnings      clean
  cargo fmt --check                                            clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
